---
description: "Generate a Mu3Library idea bank of genuinely new package directions when the next direction is unclear."
name: "개발 아이디어 뱅크"
argument-hint: "Describe the audience, package lane, or future direction you want ideas for. Leave it broad if you want repository-wide whitespace discovery"
agent: "agent"
---

Produce a Mu3Library idea bank for the described area.

- Use the `development-idea-bank` skill.
- Keep the work in pre-unit concept shaping only.
- Default to new package directions, workflow multipliers, ecosystem bridges, or adoption wedges rather than strengthening an existing feature.
- If the request starts from one pain point or one existing feature, treat it as evidence of adjacent whitespace instead of converging on that feature.
- Start from repository evidence that explains the current package intent, then build a capability map and whitespace map before proposing ideas.
- Use limited web research only after the whitespace map exists; repository constraints win.
- Generate 6 distinct ranked ideas across different buckets and keep at most one low-priority incremental baseline idea.
- Keep the top 3 ideas outside docs-only, sample-only, or helper-polish territory unless the user explicitly asks for refinement.
- Preserve the option space: do not collapse the response into a single concept brief unless the user explicitly asks to deepen one idea after seeing the bank.
- Rank the ideas using novelty, package fit, leverage, feasibility, and risk.
- End with short selection hooks or follow-up questions that help choose which idea to deepen next.
- Do not produce execution sequencing or final owner selection.
